title: F5 BIG-IP DNS (GTM) Wide-IP Query Volume and NXDOMAIN Baseline
dataSources: F5 BIG-IP DNS iRule HSL to Splunk, `sourcetype=f5:bigip:gtm:dns:request:irule` and `sourcetype=f5:bigip:gtm:dns:response:irule`
app: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP (`Splunk_TA_f5-bigip`, Splunkbase 2680)
spl:
index=network (sourcetype="f5:bigip:gtm:dns:request:irule" OR sourcetype="f5:bigip:gtm:dns:response:irule") earliest=-24h
| eval qname=coalesce(query_name, dns_qname, name)
| eval rcode=coalesce(response_code, dns_rcode, rcode)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as qps, sum(eval(rcode="NXDOMAIN" OR rcode="3")) as nxdomain by _time, wideip, qname
| eventstats avg(qps) as baseline by wideip
| where qps > baseline*3 AND qps > 1000
| sort -qps
description: Abnormal DNS query rates or sudden NXDOMAIN spikes on GTM wide IPs can indicate cache poisoning attempts, random subdomain attacks, or misconfigured delegations. Monitoring per wide IP and QNAME supports both availability and security investigations.
value: Detects DNS-based attacks and configuration regressions before they degrade global server load balancing or overwhelm resolvers.
implementation: Deploy the documented BIG-IP DNS iRules for HSL query and response logging into Splunk. Normalize `query_name` and response code fields using the TA. Establish per–wide-IP hourly baselines; tune multipliers for enterprise scale. Feed high-volume NXDOMAIN patterns into threat hunting workflows.
visualization: Time chart (queries per wide IP), Table (top QNAMEs), Single value (NXDOMAIN ratio).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP — Configure iRules for BIG-IP DNS (GTM)
Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP
